rounds.prompt: How many times more energy does a lightning bolt release than a camera flash?
rounds.fact: A camera flash releases ~0.001 kWh; a lightning bolt releases ~1–5 kWh — about 1,000–5,000× more energy, delivered in microseconds.

rounds.prompt: How many times more power does the Sun output than all human electricity generation combined?
rounds.fact: Humanity generates ~2.5 × 10¹³ W; the Sun outputs ~3.8 × 10²⁶ W — about 15 trillion× more. In one second, the Sun produces more energy than humanity uses in a million years.

rounds.prompt: How many times more energy does a nuclear bomb release than a ton of TNT?
rounds.fact: 1 tonne of TNT releases ~4.2 GJ; the Hiroshima bomb released ~63,000 GJ (15 kilotons) — 15,000× more energy.

rounds.prompt: How many times more electricity does a kettle use than an LED bulb (per hour)?
rounds.fact: An LED uses ~10W; a kettle uses ~3,000W — 300× more power. Boiling your kettle for 3 minutes uses as much energy as leaving an LED on for 15 hours.

rounds.prompt: How many times more energy does the human body use per day than a smartphone battery?
rounds.fact: A smartphone battery holds ~12 Wh; a human body uses ~2,000 kcal/day (~2,300 Wh) — about 190× more energy to keep you alive than your phone.
